questions.question: Explain Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) under E-Waste Management Rules 2022 and its importance.
keyPoints: EPR makes producers accountable for end-of-life management of their products
Producers must collect and recycle e-waste equal to their market share
Helps shift burden from government/consumers to producers
Promotes sustainable product design and circular economy

questions.question: List any five hazardous substances restricted under E-Waste Management Rules 2022.
keyPoints: Lead (Pb)
Mercury (Hg)
Cadmium (Cd)
Hexavalent Chromium (Cr6)
Poly Brominated Biphenyls (PBB)
Poly Brominated Diphenyl Ethers (PBDE)

questions.question: What are the key functions of Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) under e-waste management?
keyPoints: Registration of producers, PROs, and recyclers
Monitoring and enforcement of rules
Setting collection and recycling targets
Maintaining national database on e-waste

questions.question: Explain the role of E-Waste Exchange in India.
keyPoints: Digital platform connecting e-waste generators, collectors, dismantlers, and recyclers
Ensures transparent pricing of e-waste
Provides traceability of e-waste from collection to disposal
Facilitates formalization of the e-waste sector

questions.question: What are the penalties for violation of E-Waste Management Rules 2022?
keyPoints: First violation: Warning and direction to comply
Subsequent violations: Fine up to ₹1 lakh
Continuing violation: Additional fine up to ₹5000 per day
Severe cases: Imprisonment up to 5 years

questions.question: Describe the collection and recycling targets under EPR for producers.
keyPoints: First year: 60% collection target
2024-25: 70% collection target
2025-26: 80% collection target
Minimum 30% recycling of collected e-waste initially
60% material recovery rate from recycling

questions.question: Explain the responsibilities of consumers under E-Waste Management Rules 2022.
keyPoints: Dispose e-waste only at authorized collection points
Should not mix e-waste with municipal solid waste
Should ensure e-waste is handed over to authorized recyclers
Can participate in buy-back and exchange programs
Should follow proper handling guidelines

questions.question: Describe the step-by-step procedure for a producer to obtain EPR authorization under E-Waste Management Rules 2022.
keyPoints:
1. Register on the CPCB portal
2. Submit application with required documents
3. Provide annual action plan with collection targets
4. Submit details of partnership with PRO and recyclers
5. Submit details of collection mechanisms
6. CPCB reviews and grants authorization
7. Authorization valid for 1 year, renew annually
8. File quarterly and annual returns

questions.question: Discuss the environmental and health impacts of improper e-waste management and the measures to address them.
keyPoints:
Environmental impacts: Soil contamination, water pollution, air pollution, accumulation in food chain
Health impacts: Neurological damage, kidney failure, respiratory problems, cancer risks
Measures: Proper collection, authorized recycling, public awareness, strict enforcement
The rules aim to formalize e-waste sector and ensure scientific disposal

questions.question: Explain the role and functions of Producer Responsibility Organization (PRO) in e-waste management.
keyPoints:
PRO is an entity that assists producers in fulfilling EPR obligations
Functions: Help producers meet collection targets
Act as intermediary between producers, collectors, and recyclers
Maintain records of e-waste collected and recycled
Ensure compliance with CPCB guidelines
Help in public awareness campaigns
Assist in setting up collection infrastructure

questions.question: Discuss the challenges faced in implementation of E-Waste Management Rules 2022 and suggest solutions.
keyPoints:
Challenges: Informal sector dominance, lack of awareness, inadequate infrastructure, enforcement issues, collection targets not met
Solutions: Strengthen enforcement, promote formalization of informal sector, increase public awareness, improve collection infrastructure, provide incentives for recycling
The rules aim to address these through EPR and regulatory framework
